title: Hume AI open-sources TADA, a speech model five times faster than rivals with zero hallucinated words
summary: Hume AI has open-sourced TADA under the MIT license, a fast speech generation model that processes text and audio in sync and produced zero hallucinations in testing. The article Hume AI open-sources TADA, a speech model five times faster than rivals with zero hallucinated words appeared first on The Decoder.

title: Ai2 releases new robotics models trained entirely in simulation to skip real-world data collection
summary: Robots trained entirely in virtual worlds working in the real world without any real training data. Ai2 says it can be done. The article Ai2 releases new robotics models trained entirely in simulation to skip real-world data collection appeared first on The Decoder.

title: The Unlearning Mirage: A Dynamic Framework for Evaluating LLM Unlearning

title: LLMs can construct powerful representations and streamline sample-efficient supervised learning
summary: arXiv:2603.11679v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As real-world datasets become increasingly complex and heterogeneous, supervised learning is often bottlenecked by input representation design. Modeling multimodal data for downstream tasks, such as time-series, free text, and structured records, often requires non-trivial domain-specific engineering.
